joke: I told my computer I needed a break, and now it won’t stop sending me KitKats.
I tried to start a band with my vegetables, but they only knew how to play the beet.
I asked my pillow for advice. It said, “Let’s sleep on it.”
I bought a cactus a hat. Now it’s a little more pointed in its opinions.
I bought some socks with a hole in them. They’re really putting their foot down.
I told a joke about the ocean, but it just went over everyone’s head.
I took my turtle to the race. He said he’d get there eventually.
I tried to make a joke about elevators, but it had its ups and downs.
I tried to write a joke about taxes, but it didn’t have enough deductions.
I opened a bakery for introverts. It’s quiet, but the turnover is great.
I tried to organize my books alphabetically, but I got lost in the appendix.
I asked the detective for a joke, and he said, “I’m still working on the punch line.”
I told my umbrella a joke, and it opened right up.
I tried telling a joke to the moon, but it just went through phases.
I tried to make a joke about chemistry, but I got no reaction.
I bought a calendar. Now my days are numbered.
I tried to make a sandwich disappear, but it just vanished into the bread.
I asked my coffee for a promotion, but it said it was already brewing something bigger.
I told my chair a joke. It just sat there and cracked up.
I used to be indecisive, but now I’m not so sure.
I made friends with a paperclip. It really knows how to keep things together.
I bought a mirror with a great sense of humor. It really cracks itself up.
I asked a traffic light for relationship advice. It said, “Don’t rush.”
I asked my shadow for advice. It said, “Stick with me.”
I asked the refrigerator for a compliment. It said, “You’re looking ice.”
I entered a pun contest. No one laughed, but I still got a participation trophy.
I told a joke about semicolons, but it just kept going on and on.
I tried to catch fog yesterday. Mist.
I went to the barber and asked for a haircut that says, “I have my life together.” He gave me bangs and an apology.
I tried to write a joke about a deck of cards, but the punchline kept getting shuffled around.
